heading: Brokered deposits
label: 1831f Brokered deposits

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: An insured depository institution that is not well capitalized may not accept funds obtained, directly or indirectly, by or through any deposit broker for deposit into 1 or more deposit accounts.

children.heading: Renewals and rollovers treated as acceptance of funds
children.label: b Renewals and rollovers treated as acceptance of funds
children.text: Any renewal of an account in any troubled institution and any rollover of any amount on deposit in any such account shall be treated as an acceptance of funds by such troubled institution for purposes of subsection (a).

children.heading: Waiver authority
children.label: c Waiver authority
children.text: The Corporation may, on a case-by-case basis and upon application by an insured depository institution which is adequately capitalized (but not well capitalized), waive the applicability of subsection (a) upon a finding that the acceptance of such deposits does not constitute an unsafe or unsound practice with respect to such institution.

children.heading: Limited exception for certain conservatorships
children.label: d Limited exception for certain conservatorships

children.text: is not an unsafe or unsound practice;
is necessary to enable the institution to meet the demands of its depositors or pay its obligations in the ordinary course of business; and
is consistent with the conservator’s fiduciary duty to minimize the institution’s losses.

children.heading: Restriction on interest rate paid
children.label: e Restriction on interest rate paid
A
children.text: the terms “agent institution”, “reciprocal deposits”, and “well capitalized” have the meanings given those terms in subsection (i); and

children.label: i
children.text: under subsection (c) or (d), accepts funds obtained, directly or indirectly, by or through a deposit broker; or

children.label: ii
children.text: while acting as an agent institution under subsection (i), accepts reciprocal deposits while not well capitalized.

children.heading: Prohibition
children.label: 2 Prohibition
children.text: A covered insured depository institution may not pay a rate of interest on funds or reciprocal deposits described in paragraph (1) that, at the time that the funds or reciprocal deposits are accepted, significantly exceeds the limit set forth in paragraph (3).

children.label: A
children.text: the rate paid on deposits of similar maturity in the normal market area of the covered insured depository institution for deposits accepted in the normal market area of the covered insured depository institution; or

children.label: B
children.text: the national rate paid on deposits of comparable maturity, as established by the Corporation, for deposits accepted outside the normal market area of the covered insured depository institution.

children.heading: Additional restrictions
children.label: f Additional restrictions
children.text: The Corporation may impose, by regulation or order, such additional restrictions on the acceptance of brokered deposits by any institution as the Corporation may determine to be appropriate.

children.heading: Definitions relating to deposit broker
children.label: g Definitions relating to deposit broker
A
children.text: any person engaged in the business of placing deposits, or facilitating the placement of deposits, of third parties with insured depository institutions or the business of placing deposits with insured depository institutions for the purpose of selling interests in those deposits to third parties; and

children.label: B
children.text: an agent or trustee who establishes a deposit account to facilitate a business arrangement with an insured depository institution to use the proceeds of the account to fund a prearranged loan.

children.label: A
children.text: an insured depository institution, with respect to funds placed with that depository institution;

children.label: B
children.text: an employee of an insured depository institution, with respect to funds placed with the employing depository institution;

children.label: C
children.text: a trust department of an insured depository institution, if the trust in question has not been established for the primary purpose of placing funds with insured depository institutions;

children.label: D
children.text: the trustee of a pension or other employee benefit plan, with respect to funds of the plan;

children.label: E
children.text: a person acting as a plan administrator or an investment adviser in connection with a pension plan or other employee benefit plan provided that that person is performing managerial functions with respect to the plan;

children.label: F
children.text: the trustee of a testamentary account;

children.label: G
children.text: the trustee of an irrevocable trust (other than one described in paragraph (1)(B)), as long as the trust in question has not been established for the primary purpose of placing funds with insured depository institutions;

children.label: H
children.text: a trustee or custodian of a pension or profitsharing plan qualified under section 401(d) or 403(a) of title 26; or

children.label: I
children.text: an agent or nominee whose primary purpose is not the placement of funds with depository institutions.

children.heading: Inclusion of depository institutions engaging in certain activities
children.label: 3 Inclusion of depository institutions engaging in certain activities
children.text: Notwithstanding paragraph (2), the term “deposit broker” includes any insured depository institution that is not well capitalized (as defined in section 1831 of this title), and any employee of such institution, which engages, directly or indirectly, in the solicitation of deposits by offering rates of interest which are significantly higher than the prevailing rates of interest on deposits offered by other insured depository institutions in such depository institution’s normal market area.o

children.label: A
children.text: who is employed exclusively by the insured depository institution;

children.label: B
children.text: whose compensation is primarily in the form of a salary;

children.label: C
children.text: who does not share such employee’s compensation with a deposit broker; and

children.label: D
children.text: whose office space or place of business is used exclusively for the benefit of the insured depository institution which employs such individual.
in such institution’s normal market areas; or
in the market area in which such deposits would otherwise be accepted.

children.heading: Limited exception for reciprocal deposits
children.label: i Limited exception for reciprocal deposits
B
children.text: an amount equal to 20 percent of the total liabilities of the agent institution.

children.label: I
children.text: when most recently examined under was found to have a composite condition of outstanding or good; andsection 1820(d) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: is well capitalized;

children.label: ii
children.text: has obtained a waiver pursuant to subsection (c); or

children.label: iii
children.text: does not receive an amount of reciprocal deposits that causes the total amount of reciprocal deposits held by the agent institution to be greater than the average of the total amount of reciprocal deposits held by the agent institution on the last day of each of the 4 calendar quarters preceding the calendar quarter in which the agent institution was found not to have a composite condition of outstanding or good or was determined to be not well capitalized.

children.label: i
children.text: is submitted for placement through a deposit placement network by an agent institution; and

children.label: ii
children.text: does not consist of funds that were obtained for the agent institution, directly or indirectly, by or through a deposit broker before submission for placement through a deposit placement network.

children.heading: Deposit placement network
children.label: C Deposit placement network
children.text: The term “deposit placement network” means a network in which an insured depository institution participates, together with other insured depository institutions, for the processing and receipt of reciprocal deposits.

children.heading: Network member bank
children.label: D Network member bank
children.text: The term “network member bank” means an insured depository institution that is a member of a deposit placement network.

children.heading: Reciprocal deposits
children.label: E Reciprocal deposits
children.text: The term “reciprocal deposits” means deposits received by an agent institution through a deposit placement network with the same maturity (if any) and in the same aggregate amount as covered deposits placed by the agent institution in other network member banks.

children.heading: Well capitalized
children.label: F Well capitalized
children.text: The term “well capitalized” has the meaning given the term in section 1831(b)(1) of this title.o
